master_row.title: Modal Frequency Relation
master_row.canonical_statement: }$ = Alfvén velocity \item $\delta \mathbf{B}$ = magnetic field perturbation \end{itemize} \subsection{Topological Mode Coupling} The magnetic helicity (topological invariant) couples to acoustic modes: \begin{equation} \mathcal{H}_M = \int_V \mathbf{A} \cdot \mathbf{B} \, dV - \frac{\omega_{gyro}}{c^2} \int_V \mathbf{r} \times (\mathbf{E} \times \mathbf{B}) \, dV \end{equation} Time derivative under acoustic modulation \subsection{Type I: Alfvén Resonance Ladder} \begin{theorem}[Alfvén Mode Quantization] In a spherical cavity of radius R with permeable boundary $\mu_r$, Alfvén waves satisfy: \begin{equation} \omega_{n\ell m} = \frac{v_A}{R} \sqrt{n^2 + \frac{\ell(\ell+1)}{1 + \frac{R}{2\delta_{skin}}}} \end{equation} where n, $\ell$, m are quantum numbers encoding topological winding. \end{theorem} \textbf{Achievable Today}: Frequencies 1-100 kHz in ferrofluid systems with B $\sim$ 0.1-1 T. \subsection{Type II: Hopf Fibration Harmonics} Your rotating dipole system naturally generates Hopf fibration structure: \begin{equation} S^3 \xrightarrow{\pi} S^2, \quad \pi^{-1}(p) \cong S^1 \end{equation} The fiber circles $S^1$ correspond to magnetic field lines. Acoustic standing waves alo
